# Claude Code prompt ‚Äî [5A.2] HTML‚ÜíPDF renderer infrastructure (PR 1 of ¬ß5A.2)

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` fully and
follow every rule in it. This is **PR 1 of roadmap ¬ß5A.2**.

## Context & motivation

Roadmap ¬ß5A.2 replaces the PDFKit-based PDF generator with HTML‚ÜíPDF rendering.
PDFKit's layout engine causes the bug class Sean is hitting ‚Äî header/footer
drift, logo borders, fonts changing mid-paragraph, text overlap, the T&C
two-column layout breaking. A browser rendering engine eliminates that class
of bug and unlocks future rich-text / template-editor work.

This PR builds **only the renderer infrastructure**. The actual document
migrations (Quote, Variation, Schedule of Rates) are separate follow-up PRs,
each built against Sean's reference templates and signed off individually.

**Current state ‚Äî do not modify it in this PR:** PDF generation is PDFKit-based,
centred in `apps/api/src/modules/estimate-export/pdf/quote-pdf.builder.ts`
(1,173 lines, exports `buildQuotePdf`), consumed by `QuotePdfService`
(`apps/api/src/modules/client-quotes/quote-pdf.service.ts`). PDFKit is retired
only after the document-migration PRs land and Sean signs off. This PR adds the
new renderer **alongside** the existing code ‚Äî it does not touch it.

## Pre-work (Phase 0)

- `git fetch origin`.
- Branch off `main`: `feat/5a2-html-pdf-renderer`.
- Conflict check: for each file you plan to add/change, run
  `git log --oneline --all -- <file>`. If competing work exists, stop and report.
- Read the existing PDF code before writing anything (`quote-pdf.builder.ts`,
  `quote-pdf.service.ts`, `estimate-export.module.ts`) so the new service fits
  the codebase conventions.

## Engine decision (decided ‚Äî implement as specified)

Use **`puppeteer`** ‚Äî the full package, with bundled Chromium. The API deploys
to an Azure Web App, which runs full Chromium; `@sparticuz/chromium` is only
needed for serverless size limits and is not warranted here. Add `puppeteer`
to `apps/api` **only**, in a single `pnpm add` call, and commit the updated
`pnpm-lock.yaml` in the same commit as `package.json`.

## What to build

### 1. New module ‚Äî `PdfRenderingModule`
- Location: `apps/api/src/modules/pdf-rendering/`.
- Provides and exports `PdfRendererService` so other modules can import it.

### 2. `PdfRendererService`

Core API:
- `renderHtmlToPdf(html: string, options?: PdfRenderOptions): Promise<Buffer>` ‚Äî
  render a complete HTML document string to a PDF buffer.
- `loadTemplate(name: string): Promise<string>` ‚Äî read an HTML template file
  from the templates directory (see ¬ß3).
- `renderTemplateToPdf(name: string, data: Record<string, unknown>, options?: PdfRenderOptions): Promise<Buffer>` ‚Äî
  load a template, interpolate `data`, render. Keep interpolation minimal ‚Äî a
  small `{{key}}` substitution helper. Do **not** add a heavy templating
  framework.

`PdfRenderOptions` (all optional, with sensible defaults):
- `format` ‚Äî default `"A4"`.
- `margin` ‚Äî default per the IS PDF spec (project_instructions ¬ß11):
  **15 mm left / right, 25 mm top, 20 mm bottom**.
- `headerHtml` / `footerHtml` + `displayHeaderFooter`.
- `printBackground` ‚Äî default `true` (so brand background colours render).
- `landscape` ‚Äî default `false`.
- `timeoutMs` ‚Äî render timeout, default 30000.

Browser lifecycle (important ‚Äî get this right):
- Lazy-launch a **single shared** Chromium instance on first render; reuse it.
- Launch args **must** include `--no-sandbox`, `--disable-setuid-sandbox`,
  `--disable-dev-shm-usage` (required in CI / App Service containers).
- One fresh `page` per render; always `page.close()` in a `finally`.
- If the browser has disconnected/crashed, transparently relaunch.
- Implement `OnModuleDestroy` to close the browser cleanly.
- Guard concurrency (a simple in-flight limit or queue) so simultaneous
  renders don't exhaust memory.
- On failure throw a typed `PdfRenderError` with a clear message.

### 3. Templates directory + brand fonts
- HTML/CSS templates live in the repo on the filesystem:
  `apps/api/src/modules/pdf-rendering/templates/`.
- Bundle the IS brand fonts so output is deterministic regardless of host
  fonts: **Outfit** (body) and **Syne** (headings) ‚Äî project_instructions ¬ß5.
  Both are OFL-licensed and redistributable. Reference them via `@font-face`
  (local font files under a templates assets dir) in template CSS.
- Add ONE sample template (e.g. `sample.html`) ‚Äî an IS-branded test page that
  exercises both fonts, the brand colours, and a header/footer. This is a test
  fixture, not a real document template.
- Brand tokens (¬ß5): teal `#005B61`, orange `#FEAA6D` (interactive only),
  black `#000000`, light grey `#F6F6F6`. Mirror the CSS-variable naming used
  in `apps/web/src/styles/tokens.css`.

### 4. Build / deploy / CI
- Puppeteer downloads Chromium on install. Ensure the **GitHub Actions API
  test job** can run Puppeteer ‚Äî install the required Chromium system
  libraries in the workflow (or use Puppeteer's documented CI setup). The
  renderer test below must run and pass in CI.
- Ensure the **Azure deploy workflow** still succeeds and the deployed API has
  Chromium available (Puppeteer cache included in the build artifact, or the
  requirement documented). Do not break `pnpm build:azure`.

## Out of scope ‚Äî do NOT do in this PR
- Migrating the Quote, Variation, or Schedule-of-Rates PDFs.
- Modifying or deleting `quote-pdf.builder.ts`, `tc-text.const.ts`,
  `QuotePdfService`, or any PDFKit code.
- Any Prisma schema change / migration (this PR has none).
- New public API endpoints ‚Äî the renderer is consumed internally by future
  PRs; none is needed now.
- A `PDF_ENGINE` switch / feature flag ‚Äî that belongs with the migration PRs.

## Testing (must pass before the PR)
- Integration test for `PdfRendererService`: render the sample template, then
  assert the result is a valid PDF (starts with `%PDF-`), is non-trivial in
  size, and has ‚â•1 page (use the existing `pdfjs-dist` dependency to count
  pages). Assert it also renders successfully with custom margins and with
  header/footer enabled.
- Unit tests: `PdfRenderOptions` defaults, the template loader (including a
  not-found error path), and the `{{key}}` interpolation helper.
- The renderer test must run and pass in CI as part of the API test gate.
